key_insight: Thermal hysteresis in NiTiCu directly imprints closed-loop, history-dependent band gaps and transmission spectra in a periodic rod phononic crystal.
engineering_relevance: By tuning temperature and geometric filling fraction, engineers can switch and reposition acoustic stop bands in composite thin rods for tunable vibration or wave filtering.
method: Use a 1D periodic rod model with alternating NiTiCu and Parylene C segments.
Apply the transfer-matrix method separately on heating and cooling branches to compute Bloch bands and finite-rod transmission.
Map stop-band edges and gap closures versus temperature and filling fraction.
evidence_signal: The study reports computed band structures and transmission spectra showing hysteretic loops in the temperature–frequency plane and history-dependent finite-rod transmission.
watch_item: Predictions rely on branch-specific material data and an idealized 1D thin-rod model, so real-device performance may shift with losses, imperfect interfaces, and fabrication tolerances.
paper_title: Hysteretic Acoustic Band Structures in Shape-Memory Composite Thin Rods
